source_label_for_outputs: historical statistics for comparable VC/PE projects
default_method: Use the 2025 current-year statistic as the primary anchor. Where exact comparable 2024 and 2025 figures are available in the market benchmark reference, use the 2024/2025 two-year average. Where 2024 is unavailable or not comparable, use 2025 only and mark that internally.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: RMB/onshore 66.51%, offshore 33.49%; RMB currency 64.62%, USD 34.43%; onshore pure domestic 73.05%; offshore VIE 40.85%, direct holding 59.15%; early rounds 40.56%; state-owned/government-backed investors nearly 40% overall and over 50% in onshore deals.
benchmarks.review_use: Classify structure first. In onshore deals, check pure-domestic/FIE status, SOE/government investor requirements, and registered-capital mechanics. In offshore deals, route direct-holding vs VIE issues separately.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: overall 93.40%, RMB 94.33%, offshore 91.55%; absolute-ratio allocation 73.23%; over-allotment 56.06%. 2024/2025 average: overall 93.08%, absolute allocation 74.33%, over-allotment 56.80%.
benchmarks.review_use: Missing preemptive rights are unusual; negotiate allocation formula, over-allotment, statutory PRC LLC preemptive subscription, and exceptions by party side.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: overall 89.15%, RMB 91.49%, offshore 84.51%; ordinary-share transfer/investor ROFR 89.95%; any-shareholder transfer ROFR 9.52%; relative-ratio allocation 81.18%; over-purchase 54.12%.
benchmarks.review_use: Founder/common share transfer restrictions remain market-standard but declined in 2025; review permitted transfers, investor transfer restrictions, competitor carve-outs, and PRC LLC statutory ROFR.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: overall 88.21%, RMB 90.07%, offshore 84.51%; change-of-control special co-sale 22.46%.
benchmarks.review_use: Check sequencing with ROFR and formula breadth; investor side may ask for change-of-control full tag, while company/founder side should preserve small permitted transfers.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: overall 79.72%, RMB 86.52%, offshore 66.20%, a nine-year low; material breach trigger 94.08%; cure period 59.75%; QIPO trigger 83.43%; cross-trigger 33.73%.
benchmarks.review_use: Key exit clause, especially in RMB deals, but not near-universal. RMB deals need capital-reduction, share-transfer, collectability, founder liability, and cure-period review.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: only 16.57% of redemption clauses made the company the sole obligor; company-only was 9.84% in RMB deals and 34.04% in offshore deals. Founder involvement in redemption obligations increased.
benchmarks.review_use: Investor side should seek founder support where company redemption is uncertain. Founder side should request specified triggers, second-ranking liability, equity/equity-value caps, and no liability for non-founder-caused breaches.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: investment amount plus simple/compound interest 86.98%; fixed-multiple-only average 100%; simple interest avg 8.03%; compound interest avg 8.33%.
benchmarks.review_use: Prices materially above benchmark need negotiation and enforceability comments; align with liquidation preference and avoid cumulative high-return economics.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: overall 85.85%, RMB 85.82%, offshore 85.92%; weighted-average formula 79.12% overall, RMB 74.38%, offshore 88.52%; full ratchet 20.88%.
benchmarks.review_use: Full ratchet is investor-friendly; company side should seek weighted average and carve-outs. RMB implementation needs nominal issuance, founder transfer, cash compensation, tax, and registration review.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: overall 60.85%, RMB 58.16%, offshore 66.20%; investor-only drag approval 31.01%; founder/founder-director approval included 52.71%; time/valuation plus shareholder approval trigger 41.86%; shareholder approval only 23.26%.
benchmarks.review_use: Offshore deals use drag more often; company side should request valuation, timing, shareholder approval, and founder consent conditions. Investor side should preserve a workable trade-sale path.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: no preferred dividend 62.26%; if a preferred dividend exists, priority ranking 75.95%, dividend rate 20.25%, both 3.80%; rate bands: 5%-10% at 78.95%, 10%-15% at 15.79%.
benchmarks.review_use: Not a mandatory clause; high rates need burden comments, especially if combined with redemption and liquidation return.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: overall 83.49%, RMB 81.56%, offshore 87.32%, a nine-year low; investor veto appears in 95.48% of protective-provision clauses; investor veto threshold 6.51%; founder veto 6.21%.
benchmarks.review_use: Control reserved-matter list, veto holder, thresholds, and deadlock. Investor veto is common, but 2025 data supports careful tailoring rather than every-investor vetoes.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2024 investor director right 72.02%. 2025 report states a clear decline but does not give an extractable exact overall adoption percentage; among deals granting investor board seats, threshold use was 43.33%, RMB 55.56%, offshore 25.00%; over 90% of entitled investors appointed directors at closing; director indemnity agreements appeared in 26.32%.
benchmarks.review_use: Director rights are not automatic for every investor; threshold, observer rights, director indemnity, D&O insurance, and Articles/SHA consistency matter.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: adopted plan 31.13%, reserved/planned plan 53.30%; RMB adopted/reserved 85.11%, offshore adopted/reserved 83.10%; RMB plans used options 50% and restricted equity 48%; offshore plans used options 87.50%; 98% of RMB plans used holding platforms and all such platforms were limited partnerships.
benchmarks.review_use: Check pool size, pre/post-money treatment, approvals, leaver rules, grant form, holding-platform mechanics, and PRC tax/registration issues.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: overall 88.68%, RMB 87.23%, offshore 91.55%; participating 80.85%, capped participating 3.29%; RMB participating 89.43%, offshore participating 64.62%; investment plus interest formula 55.32%; multiple-only 24.47%; higher-of formula 18.62%; multiple avg 105.40%; simple interest avg 8.29%; compound interest avg 8.25%.
benchmarks.review_use: Participating preference is common but investor-friendly; 2025 supports non-participating or capped participating fallbacks, especially offshore. Align waterfall with Articles.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: founder transfer restriction 88.68%, RMB 92.91%, offshore 80.28%; 98.92% prohibit founder transfer during the specified period without investor consent; restricted founder equity 46.70%; vesting/release period 3-5 years in 90.91%.
benchmarks.review_use: Protect founder stability while adding permitted transfers, internal/family/trust carve-outs, reasonable leaver mechanics, and fair purchase-price rules.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: 11.32% of projects involved founder secondary sale, mostly in RMB structures; tax allocation: seller 66.67%, buyer/seller shared 8.33%, company 12.50%, unclear 12.50%; onshore LLC founder secondary sales had 100% fully paid registered capital for the transferred equity.
benchmarks.review_use: Check disclosure, tax allocation, old-share price, primary/secondary split, control implications, and unpaid-capital transfer liability.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: information rights remain around the long-term c.90% market level; information-right thresholds: offshore 28%, RMB 4.88%, average threshold 4.59%. Inspection right 66.82%; inspection thresholds: offshore 36.73%, RMB 9.78%, average threshold 4.26%.
benchmarks.review_use: Review reporting package, inspection scope, confidentiality, thresholds, non-disruption, and information firewall for strategic or competitor-sensitive investors.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: 94.81% did not set express survival periods; RMB no-survival 96.45%, offshore no-survival 91.55%; where a survival period was set, 63.64% were 1-3 years.
benchmarks.review_use: Survival limits are company-friendly. Investor side should resist broad limitation, or accept only for non-fundamental warranties with fraud/fundamental carve-outs.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: express indemnity 77.83%; indemnity cap 18.18%; basket/deductible 18.18%; neither cap nor basket 63.64%; company + founders joint/several arrangements 96.97%; founder personal cap by equity/equity value 72.67%, RMB 66.36%, offshore 88.46%.
benchmarks.review_use: Founder side should seek caps and carve-outs; investor side should protect fraud/fundamental warranties and collection path. Distinguish general company breaches from founder-caused breaches.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: 58.96% restrict investor transfers, RMB 60.99%, offshore 54.93%; absolute prohibition on transfer to company competitors rose to 25.45% in affected deals; investor investment/cooperation restrictions appeared in 5.19%.
benchmarks.review_use: Company side should review competitor and strategic-investor risks carefully. Financial investors need liquidity and affiliate/fund transfer carve-outs.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: existing/same-round MFN 25.59%; future-financing MFN 17.06%, both materially down from 2024.
benchmarks.review_use: Separate same-round from future-financing MFN; exclude senior economics from later rounds where appropriate and consider valuation/down-round triggers.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025 onshore LLCs: founders fully paid registered capital pre-closing in 37.61%; not fully paid in 62.39%, of which 98.53% were not yet due. For unpaid founders, more than half of deals required payment within the Company Law transition/statutory period, 14.71% required a shorter period, 25% were silent, and most documents/articles did not expressly address shareholder forfeiture.
benchmarks.review_use: RMB reviews must check subscription status, contribution deadlines, acceleration, unpaid-capital transfer liability, forfeiture mechanism, articles consistency, and closing/post-closing covenants.

benchmarks.benchmark: 2025: RMB deal documents used PRC law in 99.29%; offshore deal documents used Hong Kong law in over 70% and Delaware law in 16.90%; arbitration was selected in 93.87%.
benchmarks.review_use: Review arbitration body, seat, interim measures, preservation, service, consolidation, and enforcement. 涉及中国法律相关事项，请咨询执业中国律师；涉及非中国法管辖事项，请咨询相应法域的执业律师。

provenance_notice: Underlying development source records are intentionally excluded; independently verify figures before high-stakes reliance.
